# Acknowledgements {.unnumbered }

Firstly, I have to thank an old college buddy of mine who introduced me to Linux
many years ago and set me down this path of developing a passion for Linux and
open-source. Ben, if you're reading this, cheers.

I'd also like to thank the following authors who's self-published free online
textbooks both helped me acquire the skills that got me to where I am today, and
also served as inspiration for self-publishing a forever freely available online
manuscript.

* The Linux Command Line by William Schotts
* Think Python and Think Stats by Allen Downy
* Automate the Boring Stuff with Python by Al Sweigart
* Ansible for DevOps by Jeff Geerling
